model.label: Gemini 2.0 Flash
model.description: Higher variance bracket with bracket-path exploration

picks.rationale: Duke still advances in the opener because the favorite case is overwhelming.
Kentucky is kept alive, but with clear volatility.
Drake is the upset swing because the profile supports a narrow path steal.
Michigan State gets the edge from consistency.
Duke survives the left side of the tree despite the volatile South pick.
Michigan State remains a safer penultimate-round ticket than Drake.
The title hedge is Michigan State because the route feels less fragile than Duke's at the very end.

reasoning.title: Macro bracket thesis
reasoning.summary: This model prefers one extra upset pocket and then leans into a contrarian title game finish.
evidence: Drake is the main upset wedge.
The left side stays chalk enough to keep Duke live.
The final is used as a differentiator.

reasoning.title: Inputs used
reasoning.summary: The agent traded away some expected points in exchange for uniqueness in likely pool overlap spots.
evidence: Lower confidence on the title reflects intentional variance.
Semifinal structure still respects stronger seeds.
Source snapshots mostly influenced upset selection, not heavy favorites.
